36~|~What is open market operation and how does it work?~|~Answer: Open market operation is the process by which a central bank buys or sells government bonds (or bonds issued by the central bank itself) in the open market to manage liquidity in the banking system. When the central bank purchases bonds
column2: it pays banks and financial institutions
column3: thereby injecting cash into the system; this increases the money supply and tends to lower short‚ÄĎterm interest rates. Conversely
column4: when the central bank sells bonds
column5: it absorbs cash from the banks
column6: reducing the money supply and can lead to higher short‚ÄĎterm interest rates. The operation is conducted through bidding processes and may be accompanied by collateral arrangements
column7: with the goal of keeping market interest rates within a desired corridor and supporting overall monetary policy objectives.

column1: 2. **Fix the lower rate as the reference** ‚Äď keep the minimum rate unchanged

column1: *Example:* If the min is 3.85‚ÄĮ%
column2: the new maximum must not exceed 3.85‚ÄĮ%‚ÄĮ+‚ÄĮ5‚ÄĮ%‚ÄĮ=‚ÄĮ8.85‚ÄĮ%.

column1: After setting the new extreme rate

300~|~ What does RTGS system membership require?~|~Answer:
RTGS system membership requires that an entity be one of the following:
1. A licensed financial institution regulated by the Nepal Rastra Bank (NRB);
2. The Ministry of Finance; or
3. An authorised domestic entity that provides payment and clearing services.
Licensed financial institutions must submit an application using the forms provided (see Appendix‚ÄĮ1) and sign the RTGS Participant Agreement
column2: thereby obtaining authorisation from NRB to participate either directly or indirectly.

column1: 301~|~Who regulates the licensed financial institutions eligible for RTGS membership?~|~Answer: The licensed financial institutions eligible for RTGS membership are regulated by Nepal Rastra Bank (NRB).
302~|~How do licensed financial institutions apply to participate in the RTGS system?~|~Answer:
Licensed financial institutions regulated by the Nepal Rastra Bank (NRB) must apply to join the RTGS system by submitting the application forms specified in the rules.
The required submission is the completed application letter for participation in the RTGS system
column2: which is provided in Appendix‚ÄĮ1 of the RTGS System Rules. If the institution intends to participate as an indirect participant
column3: it must additionally submit the application letter for indirect participation and the appointment of its settlement agent bank using the forms in Appendix‚ÄĮ2.

column1: 303~|~How many modes of participation are there in the RTGS system?~|~Answer: Three modes of participation are specified in the RTGS system.
304~|~Who is a Direct Participant in the RTGS system?~|~Answer:

A Direct Participant is an entity that holds a settlement account in the RTGS system and has the technical capability and communication platform to send and receive payment orders and other messages directly to and from the RTGS. This includes licensed banks
column3: and authorised domestic payment‚ÄĎclearing entities that meet the membership criteria and are not relying on another participant to act as an intermediary.

column1: 305~|~What technical ability does a Direct Participant have?~|~Answer: A Direct Participant has the technical ability to send and receive payment orders and other messages to and from the RTGS system.
306~|~Who is an Indirect Participant in the RTGS system?~|~Answer:
An **Indirect Participant** is an entity that holds a settlement account in the RTGS system but does not have its own direct communication platform to send or receive payment orders. Instead
column2: it appoints a single Direct Participant as its intermediary agent to handle all communications
column4: and account monitoring on its behalf.

column1: 307~|~Can an Indirect Participant manage its account position directly?~|~Answer: No. An Indirect Participant does not have the ability to manage its account position directly in the RTGS system; it must appoint a Direct Participant as its intermediary agent to send and receive payment orders

A Special Member is an entity such as a clearing house
column3: or any organisation recognised by the Nepal Rastra Bank under that category that is allowed to connect directly to the RTGS system solely for the submission of DNS files. These entities must adhere to the terms and conditions set out in the Memorandum of Understanding with NRB and the RTGS System Rules.

345~|~When do settlements occur in the RTGS system?~|~Answer:

Settlements in the RTGS system take place only after a transfer order has been received and validated by the system.  The system will process the payment according to its priority and the order in which it was accepted.  A transfer is settled immediately if the debiting participant‚Äôs settlement account has sufficient funds (or a reservation of funds) and all of the following conditions are met:
1. The RTGS system is started and the business day has opened.
2. The settlement process is not suspended.
3. The debiting account is not locked for debit and the crediting account is not locked for credit.
4. The debiting participant‚Äôs status is active (not suspended for debits).
5. The issuer of the payment order has the rights to perform the transfer on that business day.
6. No queued transaction with a higher priority level exists.
If any of these conditions are not satisfied or the debiting account lacks sufficient funds
column2: the transfer is queued and will be settled later when the conditions become favourable.
